differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with secondary vasculitis
next_steps: Initiate therapeutic dose of Pyridoxine (Vitamin B6) supplementation
Perform comprehensive autoimmune panel including RF, CCP, ANA, ANCA, and cryoglobulins
Obtain ESR and CRP to assess for systemic inflammation
Perform skin/nerve biopsy to rule out small-vessel vasculitis
Re-evaluate for underlying inflammatory arthritis (rheumatoid arthritis) given symmetric polyarthritis
Continue physical therapy and pain management with optimization of non-opioid analgesics

differential_diagnoses: Rheumatoid arthritis with secondary rheumatoid vasculitis
Systemic vasculitis (e.g., Polyarteritis nodosa or ANCA-associated vasculitis)
Small fiber neuropathy related to systemic inflammatory disease
next_steps: Obtain inflammatory markers including ESR and CRP
Order a comprehensive rheumatology panel: Rheumatoid Factor, anti-CCP, ANA, ANCA, and complement levels (C3, C4)
Perform diagnostic joint aspiration to rule out crystalline or septic arthritis
Consult rheumatology for formal evaluation of suspected systemic inflammatory process
Perform nerve biopsy (typically sural nerve) and skin biopsy to investigate for evidence of vasculitis
Continue aggressive pain management using non-opioid strategies and optimize physical therapy support
